case_metadata.case_name: Buzeska v. Crystal Run Healthcare Physicians, LLP

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On November 13, 2019, the plaintiff Nafije Buzeska (hereinafter the injured plaintiff) went to Orange Regional Medical Center (hereinafter ORMC) to undergo a surgery
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff underwent surgery at ORMC on November 13, 2019.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Upon awakening from the surgery, the injured plaintiff experienced upper abdominal pain, blood in her urine, inability to ambulate, and inability to have a bowel movement or pass gas.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Post-surgery, plaintiff had abdominal pain, blood in urine, and other symptoms.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Despite the foregoing and a note from Cruz that the injured plaintiff should not be discharged without having passed gas, the injured plaintiff was discharged the next day.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff was discharged despite not passing gas.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On November 18, 2019, the injured plaintiff returned to ORMC, where physician Seth Judd took her back into surgery and, upon discovering that her sigmoid colon was perforated, performed a bowel resection and colostomy.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff had a perforated colon discovered on November 18, 2019.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Cruz testified that the injured plaintiff could only be discharged if there was a 'return of bowel function,' indicated by passing gas, and that doing so would be 'standard medical practice.'
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Cruz stated discharge required return of bowel function.
evidence_index.speaker: Cruz

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The Supreme Court should have denied that branch of the defendants' motion which was for summary judgment dismissing the cause of action alleging medical malpractice.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Court found summary judgment for medical malpractice should be denied.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The cause of action alleging loss of consortium is derivative in nature... the court also should have denied that branch of their motion which was for summary judgment dismissing the cause of action alleging loss of consortium.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Loss of consortium claim should not be dismissed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

verdict_detected.key_phrase: The Supreme Court should have denied that branch of the defendants' motion which was for summary judgment dismissing the cause of action alleging medical malpractice.

gaps_identified: No detailed expert analysis on causation

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent surgery and experienced complications post-operatively.

reveal.result_summary: Patient should not be discharged without passing gas
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates need for monitoring bowel function
reveal.decision_impact: Should delay discharge until bowel function returns

options.description: Discharge patient after confirming return of bowel function
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard practice requires confirmation of bowel function before discharge

options.description: Discharge patient without confirming return of bowel function
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient was discharged despite not passing gas

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant discharged patient without confirming return of bowel function

expected_action_court.description: Patient should not have been discharged without passing gas
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony and standard medical practice

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Discharge without confirming bowel function led to complications
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been monitored until bowel function returned
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard medical practice as testified by expert

decision_points.prompt: What should be the immediate management upon return?

options.description: Immediate surgical intervention to address suspected perforation
options.clinical_reasoning: Perforation suspected, requires urgent surgical management
Conservative management inappropriate for suspected perforation

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant performed immediate surgery upon return

explanation.why_defendant_correct: Immediate surgery was necessary to address perforation
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Surgery was correctly performed upon return

timeline_phases.name: Initial Surgery
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes initial surgery at ORMC

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed a perforated colon requiring emergency surgery

key_findings: Breach of duty in discharging patient without confirming bowel function
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented perforation

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to confirm bowel function before discharge

factual_timeline.event: Patient returns to ORMC with complications

allegations.allegation: Failure to confirm bowel function before discharge

court_findings.finding: Defendant breached duty of care by discharging patient prematurely
